page: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 4245P processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 12 physical cores and 12 virtual cores operating at a base clock speed of 3.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 1920 GB NVMe SSD. No GPU accelerators are included. The server also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, this server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The PassMark memory score also ranks in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall within the average range. Large block memory write bandwidth represents a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Small-model LLM inference text generation is highly efficient, reaching top-tier speeds, while medium-model inference remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal configuration offers high resource density and dedicated CPU execution without virtualization overhead. The combination of high-frequency single-core execution and fast local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for single-threaded processing tasks, database hosting, and lightweight machine learning inference. It is also well-suited for static web serving and general-purpose utility workloads requiring dedicated hardware resources.
description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 4245P processor with 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at 3.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression speeds, and memory scores, though multi-core execution and database operations perform at average levels. Large-block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is highly effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources, such as database hosting, single-threaded data processing, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 12-core AMD EPYC 4245P CPU, 32 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-frequency single-core performance with fast local NVMe storage for demanding workloads.

Bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD local storage and 32.0 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds
